はー、仕事終わりの１杯は格別じゃな！: Ahh... nothing beats a drink after work!
こっちはまだまだ仕事があるってのに、いい身分だな。: I've still got work to do, but you're living the good life.
ジェンマさんは採掘から戻ったばかりだから。: Gemma just got back from mining.
いい仕事のあとはいい酒と煙草、そう決まっておる。<br>小僧にもそのうち、正しい:
After a good day's work, a good drink and tobacco are a must.
Boy, I'll teach you the proper way to drink one of these days.
……少しお酒の量が多いようですが、よろしいのですか？: It seems you're drinking a lot. Are you sure that's alright?
いいんじゃいいんじゃ、もっと出さんか！: It's fine, it's fine! Keep it coming!
ジェンマさん、元気そうに振る舞ってるけど……、<br>少し無理しているようには見:
Gemma's acting cheerful, but don't you think she seems like she's
pushing herself?
（ジェンマのやつ、俺たちに弱いところなんて<br>見せたくないんだろうけど……）: (Gemma probably doesn't want to show us her weakness, but...)
放っておくつもりで？: Are you going to leave her alone?
おお、フェリシオーネ。<br>頼まれていた仕事は終えておるぞ。: Oh, Felicione. I've finished the work you asked for.
ん、確認したわ。<br>どれも使えない、あれじゃ駄目。: Mm, I checked. None of them are usable—they are no good.
な、なんじゃと！？<br>おかしなことを言うでない、お主の依頼通りの品を納品した:
Wha-what! Don't say ridiculous things! I delivered exactly what you
ordered!
わたしが依頼したのは、高純度の結晶体。<br>どれも含有量が規定を満たしてない。:
I ordered high-purity crystals. None of them meet the specified
content.
見た目だけ綺麗な出来損ないね。<br>わたしの研究素材には不適格よ。:
They are defective, just pretty on the surface. They are not suitable
for my research materials.
フェリシオーネは手にした袋からたくさんの結晶を取り出した。: Felicione took many crystals from the bag she carried.
ワシがそんなミスをするはずが……。<br>い、いや、これは……これも、これもか…:
There's no way I'd make a mistake like that... N-no, this... this one
too, and this...!
……うむ、粗悪品がいくつも混ざっておる。<br>すまん、ワシの責任じゃ。: Hmm... there are several defective ones mixed in. Sorry, it's my fault.
どうしたの、ジェンマ。<br>あなたらしくないミスね。: What is the matter, Gemma? A mistake like that is not like you.
……本当にすまぬ。<br>すぐに採掘へ向かおう。: I truly apologize. I'll head to the mine right away.
必要ないわ。<br>もう別の人に依頼を出したから。: That will not be necessary. I have already commissioned someone else.
そうか……ワシは要らん、か。: I see... so I'm not needed, huh.
……本当にどうしたの？<br>また頼むから、次までに調子を戻しておいて。:
What is really the matter? I will ask you again, so please be back to
your usual self by next time.
あなた、何か知ってるのね。: You know something, I take it.
思い当たることはある。<br>絶対とは言い切れないがな。: I have a suspicion. Can't say for certain, though.
おいっ！？<br>俺に丸投げかっ！？: Hey! Are you leaving it all to me?
部下の管理は司令官の仕事。: Managing subordinates is the Commander's job.
ほとんど同じことがお前にも言えるだろうが。<br>ペルディオンの仲間だろ、なんと: The same goes for you. She's from Perdion, like you, so help her out.
……？<br>わたしにできると思う？: ...? Do you think I can do something?
……確かにそうだな、と納得はしたが。<br>お前はそれでいいのかよ……: You've got a point, I guess, but are you okay with that...?
まあいい、ジェンマのことは俺も動いていた。<br>フェリシオーネ、少し力を貸して:
Anyway, I've already been looking into the Gemma situation. Felicione,
I need your help.
ふう……いかんのう。<br>このところ失敗が多い……そろそろ引退するべきじゃろう:
*sigh*... this won't do. I've been making too many mistakes lately...
maybe it's time for me to retire...?
む、どうした、小僧。<br>ワシに何ぞ用か？: Hmm, what is it, boy? Did you need something?
ちょっとデートに付き合ってほしいと思ってな。: I was wondering if you'd come on a date with me.
なんだと……！？<br>でえとじゃと……！？: What the—! A date? With me?
ふっ、この前の街歩きでワシの魅力にやられてしもうたか？<br>いいじゃろういいじ:
Heh, did you fall for my charm on our stroll the other day? Fine, fine,
I'm feeling generous—I'll come along!
ま、そういうことにしといてやる。<br>付き合ってくれ。: Sure, let's go with that. Come on.
（……空元気、だな。<br>まあ、今はそれでいい）: (...Just putting on a brave face. Well, that's fine for now.)
でぇとと言って付き合わせる先が職人街とは。<br>なんとも色気がないのう。:
You call it a date but take me to the artisan district? How
unromantic.
資材の仕入れに新しい店を探してる。<br>この手のことで頼るならジェンマだと思っ:
I'm looking for new shops for supplies. I figured you're the one to ask
for this kind of thing.
小僧にでえとぷらんを期待するのも酷じゃ。<br>まあ、構わんだろう。: Expecting a date plan from a boy is too much. Well, I don't mind.
しかし教える以上はすべて学んでもらうからの！<br>覚悟してついて来るんじゃぞ～:
But if I'm teaching, I'll make you learn everything! Be prepared and
follow me, got it?
ほ、ほどほどで頼みたいんだが……: Uh, I'd appreciate it if you kept it moderate...
では軽い問題を出してやろう。<br>どうしてこの街に多くの工房が店を構えているか:
Then I'll give you an easy one. Do you know why so many workshops
have stores in this town?
ここに需要があるから……ってだけじゃなさそうだな。<br>恐らくは、ここにしかな:
It's not just because there's demand here. Probably because people
are after things that can only be found here...
ほっほっほ、優秀ではないか。: Ho ho ho, not bad. You're sharp.
この街が栄える理由はな、大穴から採れる希少な、<br>場合によっては未発見の素材:
The reason this town thrives is that rare materials—sometimes even
undiscovered ones—mined from the Abyss are available here first.
この街に住む職人連中は、<br>好奇心と探究心に満ちた命知らずばかり、ということ:
The artisans who live here are all daredevils full of curiosity and a
spirit of inquiry.
命がけで好奇心を満たしに来てるやつらだもんな。<br>頼りしていいんだか、呆れれ:
They're folks who risk their lives to satisfy their curiosity. I don't
know if I should rely on them or be exasperated...
そしてもう１つ。<br>この街で仕入れをするのなら、気をつけるべきことがある。:
And one more thing. If you're going to source materials in this town,
there's something you need to watch out for.
命知らずの街だからこそ、様々な商人が集まる。<br>さて、どういった店を避けるべ:
Precisely because it's a town of daredevils, all sorts of merchants
gather. Now, what kind of shops should you stay away from?
さっきの話を踏まえれば、<br>好奇心に支配されて貴重な素材の中抜きをする店って:
Based on what you just said, shops driven by curiosity that exploit
valuable materials would be a problem.
ふむ、司令官らしい答えじゃのう。<br>それも１つじゃが、また別だ。:
Hmph, an answer worthy of the Commander. That's one way, but
there's another.
いいか、この街に来るものが抱くのは好奇心と探究心だけではない。<br>――成り上:
Listen, people who come here aren't driven only by curiosity and
knowledge. They've got ambition—the will to climb to the top.
技術もない、知識も足りない、しかし野心はある。<br>そんなやつも少なくはないん:
They've got no skills, not enough knowledge, but they've got
ambition. There are plenty of folks like that.
野心があるだけで実力不足の商人か。<br>他の街から流れてきた、口だけのペテン師:
Merchants who are all ambition and no skill? I bet there are
smooth-talking swindlers who've washed up from other towns mixed in too.
うむ！ まさに玉石混交のこの街では、<br>確かな目利きが必要ということじゃ！:
Exactly! In this city, where it's a true mix of gems and stones, you
need a sharp eye to tell the difference.
その見分けかた、ワシが小僧に仕込んでやるわい！<br>なんせこの辺りの職人とはい:
I'll teach you that skill, my lad! After all, I'm always out drinking with
the local craftsmen!
……野心を抱いてこの街に来た若者は、<br>こういうベテランに叩き直されるんだろ:
So the young ones who come to this city with ambition... they get
knocked into shape by veterans like this, huh.
何をブツブツ言っておる！<br>今日中にすべての店をまわるぞ、ついてこい！:
What are you grumbling about! We're going to hit every shop today!
Follow me!
了解だ、勉強させてもらうよ。: Got it. I'll make sure to learn a thing or two.
１日歩いた程度で情けないのう。<br>それで司令官が務まるのか？:
Pathetic, getting tired after just one day of walking! Can you even
call yourself the Commander like that?
俺の仕事は肉体労働じゃないんだよ！<br>ジェンマの話を聞くだけで精一杯だ！:
My job isn't manual labor! It takes everything I've got just to keep up
with your stories, Gemma!
ほっほっほ、しっかり覚えておくんじゃぞ。: Ho ho ho, make sure you remember it well, lad.
将来有望な若者をからかうのは、ワシらの趣味のようなもんだ。<br>これまでに得た:
Teasing promising youngsters is a bit of a hobby of mine. I want to
pass on the knowledge I've gained over the years.
……そう言って、ワシのほうが長く生きてしまうのじゃがな。: ...And yet, it seems I'm the one who always ends up living longer.
ドワーフはかなり長く生きると聞いてる。<br>友人や教え子を失ったこともあるんだ:
I've heard dwarves live for a very long time. You must have seen off
many friends and students.
ああ……何人もの友を見送ってきた。: Yeah... I've seen a lot of friends off.
誰もが己の人生を生き抜いた結果だ、惜しんではいかん。<br>……分かっていても、:
Everyone lived their lives to the fullest, so it's not something I should
regret. ...I know that, but it's still hard to bear.
ジェンマは並々と酒の注がれたジョッキを一気に飲み干した。: Gemma paused, then drained the brimming beer stein in a single gulp.
はー、いかんいかん！<br>ワシとしたことが、若いもんに湿っぽい酒を飲ませるなど:
*sigh*, no, no! Making a young lad drink such a somber toast is no way
for a veteran like me to act!
なんだよ、俺には本音を話す価値もないか？<br>ジェンマの歩んできた道ってのを学:
What's this? You don't think I'm worth hearing your true feelings? Let
me learn about the path you've walked, Gemma.
……言うたな、小僧？<br>ならば限界まで付き合ってもらおうではないか！:
...You said that, did you, boy? Then I'll have you keep me company to
the very end!
さあ飲むぞ！ 今日はワシのおごりじゃ！: Come on, drink up! Today it's on me!
……ルディア、俺のは途中から酒を抜いといてくれ。: ...Ludia, could you start watering down my drink partway through?
ジェンマさん、すっかり酔いつぶれちゃったわね。<br>このまま泊まっていっても構:
Gemma's completely passed out drunk. You're welcome to stay the
night, you know?
いいや、連れて帰るよ。<br>ほらジェンマ、背中につかまれ。: No, I'll take her home. Come on, Gemma, hold onto my back.
――夜の街で背負ったジェンマは少し酒臭く、<br>想像したよりもずっと軽い。:
As I carried her through the night streets, Gemma smelled faintly of
liquor and felt far lighter than I'd imagined.
どうしてみんな先に死んでいくんじゃ……: Why does everyone die before me...
この小さな体にどれだけの別れを積み重ねてきたのか、<br>想像もできなかった――:
I couldn't even imagine how many goodbyes that small body had
carried.
ワシを置いていくな……。<br>勝手にいなくなるのは、やめてくれ……: Don't leave me... Please don't disappear on me...
